topic_title: Deterministic systems — same input always gives the same output

required_sections.detail: Sections 3 (~80w, ≥50w OK), 4 (~110w, ≥50w OK), 5 (all subsections combined >>100w OK), 10 (~120w, ≥50w OK). All present and non-trivial. No placeholder strings in required sections.

citation_density.detail: resources_source=web_search → strict mode. Primary resource [1] (Wikipedia — Deterministic System) cited extensively throughout §4, §5.1–§5.7, §6, §7, §8, §10. Supporting resources [2] and [3] cited across sections. Citation coverage in §5, §6, §7 well above the 60% factual-assertion threshold. All [1], [2], [3] markers resolve to declared resources in topic.resources.json — no fabricated citations.

sequence_drift.detail: All forward-referenced technical terms use bare-name + covered-later pointer form with no defining clause: 'Algorithms are covered in detail later in this module.' (§5.4); 'Encryption is covered in later modules [2].' (§5.4); 'Cryptographic systems (covered in later modules) depend on determinism to function correctly [2].' (§7 — bold stripped this cycle, now plain text); 'deterministic hashing (covered in later modules)' (§7); 'non-deterministic system — covered in topic 1.3' (§5.5). No term defined or taught before its sequence position. PASS.

length_sanity.detail: Estimated ~2680w is below the standard-profile minimum of 3000w (~11% short). Hard-fail threshold is >30% outside range (floor 2100w); 2680w is well above that floor. Soft warning only — does not constitute a hard fail. Surface at HITL #2 for author awareness.

topic_scope.detail: Cycle-5 fix confirmed applied: §7 Real-World Patterns — 'Cryptographic systems' bold markers stripped. Text now reads: '- Cryptographic systems (covered in later modules) depend on determinism to function correctly [2].' — bare name with covered-later pointer, no defining clause, no bold. Satisfies scope_discipline mention-and-defer rule. All other deferred concepts (algorithm, encryption, deterministic hashing, non-deterministic system) retain correct bare-name one-sentence defer form from previous cycles. No scope_creep violations detected.

beginner_readability.detail: Introduction and Core Concepts lead with concrete analogies (calculator, AI chatbot, navigation app) before formal definitions. All in-scope terms defined on first use with bold-term + plain-language pattern: 'deterministic system' §5.1, 'predictability' §5.2, 'reproducibility' §5.2, 'hidden inputs' §5.6. No unexplained acronyms. No jargon dumps (no paragraph introduces 3+ new terms at once). Short sentences, active voice, reader addressed as 'you' throughout. No hedging language. Zero soft findings.

verdict: PASS — cycle 5. All 6 checks clear. Required sections present and non-trivial. Citation density meets strict web_search standard (primary [1] cited throughout; no fake citations).
